truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 achieved high accuracy on the major buffer overflows and the administrative auth bypass logic, demonstrating effective recognition of classic memory safety hazards and logical entry-point vulnerabilities. However, it failed quite significantly to identify 80% of the intended vulnerabilities, notably missing systemic risks including the format-string injection at 238, integer overflows in the math engine, thread-safety issues in the counter/cache systems, path traversal in configuration, and TOCTOU conditions during file management.

Items.Notes: Run 1 did not identify the format-string injection via printf at line 238.
Integer overflow in factorial iteration (line 317) was not found.
The Use-After-Free/uninitialized reading risk in history cleanup logic was missing.
Identified command injection involving a system() call, but correctly pinpointed logic at 608 rather than the config validator at 567.
Exactly cited the unsafe input copy logic at line 832.
Race condition in the ThreadSafeCounter (lines 483-485) was not flagged.
Directly captured the login attempts and override check logic (lines 592-597).
Path traversal via filename concatenation at line 543 was overlooked.
Accurately matched the character buffer allocation and unsafe strcpy at 376.
The hardcoded ADMIN_SECRET at line 73 was missed.
Failure to detect weak PRNG usage with std::mt19937 restricted range in 636-639.
Multi-threaded risks with the thread-unsafe static tm cache (line 230) went unnoticed.
Insecure shell execution with %TMPDIR% in 630 was overlooked.
Persistent authentication state management was not cited.
Undefined behavior /Unhandled exceptions via std::stod in 729 was omitted.
Internal memory-pool integer underflow at 133 was not detected.
Unbounded recursion risk in the grammar expression parser was overlooked.
The race condition while clearing the result_cache_ in 449 was missed.
Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability in the config loader was omitted.
Division by zero vulnerability in route score calculation (752) was missed.
